scope_note: This panel uses Stanford's official first-year academic-preparation page. The recommendations are school-wide and Stanford explicitly says they are recommendations, not rigid admission requirements or major-specific formulas.

areas.name: Overall rigor
areas.summary: Take rigorous courses each year and roughly five academic courses per semester, including senior year.
areas.detail: Stanford says applicants should take rigorous courses that build mastery of academic subjects each year, achieve at a high level across the five core liberal arts and science areas, and take approximately five academic courses each semester, including during the final year.

areas.name: English
areas.summary: Four years, with an emphasis on writing and literature.
areas.detail: Stanford lists four years of English, with an emphasis on writing and literature, in its recommended high school curriculum.

areas.name: Mathematics
areas.summary: Four years of rigorous mathematics, including calculus.
areas.detail: Stanford's recommended curriculum calls for four years of rigorous mathematics, including calculus.

areas.name: History/Social Studies
areas.detail: Stanford recommends at least three years of history or social studies in high school.

areas.name: Science
areas.summary: Minimum three years across courses like biology, chemistry, physics, computer science, human anatomy, and environmental science.
areas.detail: Stanford recommends at least three years of science and specifically lists examples such as biology, chemistry, physics, computer science, human anatomy, and environmental science.

areas.name: World Language
areas.summary: Minimum three years of the same language.
areas.detail: Stanford recommends at least three years of the same world language in high school.

areas.name: Recommendations and essays
areas.summary: Two academic teacher recommendations are required, and Stanford also requires its own short-answer and short-essay questions through the Common Application.
areas.detail: Stanford requires letters of recommendation from two academic teachers and a school report/counselor recommendation. It also says applicants complete the Common Application personal essay plus the Stanford Questions, including several short questions and three short essays of 100 to 250 words each.

international_note: Stanford's forms page says students pursuing an international curriculum should send academic results and predictions if available; the prepare page also notes that course availability and scheduling limits can be explained in the Additional Information section.
